improvement_directive: Strengthen thm:clique-block-minimax-comparison from equal-size cliques to known unequal partial-interference blocks, constructing a block-adaptive interval and matching lower bound at scale sqrt(log(1/alpha) sum_l (r_l/n)^2/q_l). Establish this for conventional alpha <= 1/4 with moderate effective-count conditions, eliminating the alpha < exp(-1024) restriction.
critique: The note proves a genuine same-class rate sandwich, but only for a canonical equal-size clique-block experiment and only in the extreme regime alpha < exp(-1024) with Bq at least 4096 log(1/alpha). The upper bound is a direct causal specialization of established fractional-color Bernstein concentration, while the lower bound transports a homogeneous witness to a standard random-revelation/binomial problem rather than supplying a new lower-bound principle. Consequently, the delivered novelty is the careful alignment of two known mechanisms in a narrow benchmark, not a field-level method or broadly usable characterization. The framing accurately states these limitations and does not falsely claim general-network optimality or separate necessity of the dependence and positivity penalties.
